bluesky.text: Hormuz Risk Clock, 10 Mar 2026. Transit flow remains below 10% of pre-conflict levels, and MARAD still says vessels should keep clear if possible. Working priors: reopening 25%, effective closure 55%, wider escalation 20%.

image.alt: Strait of Hormuz Risk Clock v4 for 10 March 2026 showing severe transit disruption, high attack tempo, and limited bypass capacity

thread: Navigation integrity stays impaired by persistent GNSS/GPS and AIS disruption.
Bypass capacity remains limited at roughly 3.5-5.5 mb/d versus about 20 mb/d normal Hormuz flow.

reddit.title: As of: 2026-03-10T00:00:00Z
reddit.body:
# Hormuz Risk Clock Snapshot

As of: 2026-03-10T00:00:00Z

## States
- **transit_flow**: 4/4 (worsening) — IEA says export volumes remain below 10% of pre-conflict levels
- **attack_tempo**: 4/4 (flat-high) — MARAD 2026-001A says vessels should keep clear if possible
- **insurance_availability**: 4/4 (flat-high) — cover scarce, repriced, or withdrawn
- **navigation_integrity**: 3/4 (flat-high) — persistent GNSS/GPS and AIS disruption
- **bypass_capacity**: 3/4 (flat) — 3.5–5.5 mb/d alt capacity vs ~20 mb/d Hormuz
- **asia_buffer_stress**: 1/4 (rising) — stress emerging, not yet acute everywhere

## Branch priors
- **reopening**: 25%
- **effective_closure**: 55%
- **wider_escalation**: 20%
